biases.name: Confirmation Bias
biases.definition: Seeking or interpreting information that confirms existing beliefs while discounting contradictory evidence.

biases.name: Anchoring Bias
biases.definition: Over-relying on the first piece of information encountered when making judgments.
detectionSignals: first number or impression drove the decision

biases.name: Sunk Cost Fallacy
biases.definition: Continuing a course of action because of previously invested resources rather than future value.
detectionSignals: already invested too much to stop

biases.name: Survivorship Bias
biases.definition: Focusing on surviving examples while overlooking failures that are not visible.

biases.name: Availability Heuristic
biases.definition: Overestimating the likelihood of events that are more easily recalled from memory.
detectionSignals: based on how easily examples came to mind

biases.name: Halo Effect
biases.definition: Allowing one positive trait to influence overall judgment of a person or situation.

biases.name: Negativity Bias
biases.definition: Giving more weight to negative experiences or information than positive ones.

biases.name: Self-Serving Bias
biases.definition: Attributing successes to oneself and failures to external factors.
detectionSignals: blamed others or circumstances for bad outcomes

biases.name: Optimism Bias
biases.definition: Believing that one is less likely to experience negative events than others.

biases.name: Hindsight Bias
biases.definition: Believing past events were more predictable after they have already occurred.

biases.name: Dunning-Kruger Effect
biases.definition: Overestimating one's own competence in areas of low skill or knowledge.

biases.name: Fundamental Attribution Error
biases.definition: Overemphasizing personality-based explanations for others' behavior while underemphasizing situational factors.

biases.name: Bandwagon Effect
biases.definition: Adopting beliefs or behaviors because many others do the same.

biases.name: Cherry-Picking
biases.definition: Selectively choosing evidence that supports one's position while ignoring contradictory data.

biases.name: Framing Effect
biases.definition: Drawing different conclusions from the same information depending on how it is presented.
detectionSignals: reacted differently to same data presented differently

biases.name: Gambler's Fallacy
biases.definition: Believing that past independent events affect the probability of future ones.
detectionSignals: due for a win after a streak
expected a reversal based on past outcomes

biases.name: Just-World Hypothesis
biases.definition: Assuming that people get what they deserve and deserve what they get.

biases.name: Moral Licensing
biases.definition: Allowing oneself to act immorally after previously acting morally.
detectionSignals: I earned the right to do this

biases.name: Overconfidence Effect
biases.definition: Having excessive confidence in one's own answers or judgments beyond actual accuracy.

biases.name: Placebo Effect
biases.definition: Experiencing real effects from a treatment or intervention due to belief rather than its actual mechanism.
detectionSignals: believed it worked so it did

biases.name: Reactance
biases.definition: Adopting a contrary stance when one's freedom of choice is threatened or restricted.
detectionSignals: pushed back when told what to do
wanted the opposite of what was prescribed

biases.name: Selection Bias
biases.definition: Drawing conclusions from a non-representative sample due to flawed selection process.
detectionSignals: sample did not represent the whole
ignored those who were not included

biases.name: Spotlight Effect
biases.definition: Overestimating how much others notice or remember about one's appearance or actions.
detectionSignals: overestimated how noticeable the mistake was

biases.name: Status Quo Bias
biases.definition: Preferring things to stay the same rather than change, even when change is beneficial.

biases.name: Stereotyping
biases.definition: Applying generalized beliefs about a group to an individual without individual assessment.

biases.name: Temporal Discounting
biases.definition: Preferring smaller immediate rewards over larger delayed ones.
detectionSignals: chose short-term gain over long-term benefit

biases.name: Third-Person Effect
biases.definition: Believing that media or persuasive messages affect others more than oneself.
detectionSignals: others are influenced but I am not

biases.name: Ultimate Attribution Error
biases.definition: Attributing negative outgroup behavior to disposition and positive behavior to luck or exception.
detectionSignals: their failures are because of who they are

biases.name: Zero-Risk Bias
biases.definition: Preferring to eliminate one risk entirely rather than reduce multiple risks significantly.
detectionSignals: preferred certainty over better overall outcome
